summary: Standalone experiment for testing whether a frozen-Qwen typed-bytecode compiler improves when it learns the VM consequences of its own proposed programs during repair distillation.
title: Qwen In-Policy VM-ECHO Distillation

summary: Standalone experiment for an iterative hidden-program repair policy attached to a frozen Qwen numeric compiler. The policy receives the current compiled program trace, predicts sparse slot edits, and is evaluated over multiple repair iterations.
title: Qwen Iterative Repair Policy

summary: Standalone experiment testing whether a constrained stack-ABI compiler still works when the primitive library becomes large and when tasks require nested sub-procedures rather than only linear chains.
title: Qwen Large ABI Nested Compiler

summary: This experiment tests whether a Qwen 4B backbone can write a compact latent program into fixed register slots, execute that program with a differentiable modular-arithmetic runtime, and improve by selecting among multiple latent candidate programs.
title: Qwen Latent Beam Program Compiler

summary: Standalone experiment for testing whether a frozen model can improve text-transformation accuracy by choosing which unlabeled examples should be revealed before answering held-out rows.
title: Qwen Learned Active Interrogation

summary: This experiment trains a small non-oracle verifier to rerank local repairs of programs compiled by a fixed Qwen-attached numeric compiler.
title: Qwen Learned Repair Verifier

summary: Standalone QLoRA-attached parser/compiler experiment.
title: Qwen LoRA Parser Compiler

summary: Standalone experiment for training a Qwen 4B model, with QLoRA adapters, to compile natural-language tasks into executable typed bytecode.
title: Qwen LoRA Typed-Bytecode Trace Compiler

summary: This standalone experiment tests whether a learned candidate-trace verifier can select better hidden-VM programs from a frozen Qwen-attached mixed-domain compiler. The compiler proposes an executable VM trace; the verifier reranks local candidate traces using only prompt/compiler/execution-derived features at test time.
title: Qwen Mixed-Domain Trace Verifier

summary: Standalone experiment directory for candidate-supported deterministic program selection.
title: Noisy Row Program Crystallizer

summary: Standalone numeric-copy compiler experiment for a Qwen causal language model.
title: Qwen Numeric-Copy Compiler

summary: This experiment tests whether verified local program repairs can be distilled into the Qwen-attached compiler policy itself.
title: Qwen On-Policy Repair-to-Compiler

summary: Standalone experiment for testing whether a supervised acquisition-row scorer can choose useful clarifying examples for text-transformation tasks.
title: Qwen Oracle-Distilled Acquisition Policy

summary: Can a model choose the more task-consistent full output table when shown examples, query rows, and two candidate tables?
title: Pairwise Table Judge

summary: Standalone experiment for prefix-level verification and typed beam search over executable bytecode.
title: Qwen Prefix-State Process Verifier

summary: This standalone experiment tests whether a local 4B model can compile deterministic office-style tasks into executable programs whose interpreter result, not an emitted answer token, determines correctness.
title: Qwen Program-Only Executable ABI

summary: This experiment trains a non-oracle repair selector for a frozen Qwen-attached numeric program compiler. Each prompt is compiled into an executable modular-arithmetic program, local candidate repairs are enumerated, and a small transformer verifier chooses among candidate execution traces without access to the true answer or true state trajectory at test time.
title: Qwen Progressive Repair Compiler

summary: Standalone experiment testing whether a frozen deterministic transformation ABI covers public Microsoft PROSE `Transformation.Text` tasks under within-task held-out validation.
title: Qwen Public PROSE ABI Gate

summary: Standalone experiment for testing whether a Qwen 4B model can learn to execute Python-shaped mini-programs using private latent compute positions instead of emitting an explicit chain-of-thought trace.
title: Qwen Python-Shaped Silent Executor

summary: Standalone experiment for testing whether Qwen can select executable repair candidates when the task and candidate are rendered as readable pseudocode and execution text.
title: Qwen Readable Candidate Verifier

summary: Standalone experiment testing whether a fixed office-data ABI covers real-style deterministic tasks that were not generated from the ABI.
title: Qwen Real Task ABI Coverage Gate

summary: Standalone experiment for testing whether a Qwen-attached typed-bytecode compiler improves when the model is trained as a repeated repair transition operator instead of a one-shot program generator.
title: Qwen Recurrent VM Repair Policy

summary: Standalone experiment for inducing task-local executable transformation programs from sparse examples.
title: Qwen Recursive Ephemeral Program Induction

summary: Standalone experiment testing whether recursive task decomposition improves task-level consistency on public text-transformation tasks.
title: Qwen Recursive Task Decomposition

summary: This standalone experiment tests whether a Qwen-attached model can write a program into fixed register tokens and have that program executed by a fixed cyclic runtime. The bridge reads only register-token hidden states. It predicts an initial residue, primitive operation routes, and operation arguments. A deterministic modulo-97 runtime executes the predicted program.
title: Qwen Register-Token Structured Runtime

summary: This standalone experiment tests whether a Qwen-attached model can write an executable modular-arithmetic program into a fixed bank of appended register tokens. A trainable bridge reads only those register hidden states, predicts an initial value plus per-step operations and arguments, and an invisible executor runs the predicted program modulo 97.
title: Qwen Register-Token Latent Compiler

summary: This experiment tests whether a learned verifier can improve a Qwen3-4B register-program compiler by searching local edits around the compiled latent program and selecting a better execution trace.
title: Qwen Register Trace Refiner

summary: This standalone experiment trains `Qwen/Qwen3-4B` as a recurrent controller for a typed bytecode VM.
title: Qwen Search-Augmented Rollout Distillation

summary: Standalone experiment for semantic reachability supervision over executable bytecode prefixes.
title: Qwen Semantic Prefix Value Model

summary: This experiment tests whether a shared token parser can read a frozen Qwen hidden sequence, recover ordered program symbols, and configure an executable latent modular program without token-span inputs at inference time.
title: Qwen Shared Parser Compiler

summary: This standalone experiment tests whether local repair headroom can be distilled into a deployable gated slot editor for a frozen Qwen-attached numeric compiler. The compiler emits an executable modular-arithmetic program. Offline local repair search supplies corrected-program labels when available, and the editor learns to decide which init/op/arg slots to edit plus what replacement values to emit from the base compiler trace.
title: Qwen Slot Repair Distillation

summary: Standalone paired paraphrase slot-stability experiment for a Qwen numeric-copy compiler.
title: Qwen Slot-Stability Compiler

summary: This experiment tests whether a small trainable latent compiler can read a frozen Qwen hidden sequence and configure an executable modular program without being given token-span features for the numeric values or operation words.
title: Qwen Span-Free Compiler

summary: Standalone intermediate-state ladder experiment for a Qwen numeric-copy compiler.
title: Qwen State-Ladder Compiler

summary: This standalone experiment tests why a tuned executable latent compiler learns length-24 modular programs.
title: Qwen Structural Compiler Attribution Ablation

summary: This experiment tests whether an executable latent program compiler can be expanded structurally from short chains to longer chains without using beam search, candidate reranking, or text program generation.
title: Qwen Structural Latent Compiler Expansion

summary: This experiment tests a frozen Qwen encoder attached to a structured latent executor. The trainable bridge compiles text into modular program symbols, then an invisible executor runs the compiled program to produce the answer.
title: Qwen Structured Bridge Experiment

summary: Standalone experiment testing whether a support-contrastive LoRA objective makes Qwen3-4B bind text-transformation answers to the provided support examples, rather than only adapting to the prompt format or task family.
title: Qwen Support-Contrastive Meta-ICL

summary: Standalone experiment for testing whether a learned tail-repair critic can turn mostly-correct length-24 modular programs into stable exact executable programs.
title: Qwen Tail Repair Stability Critic

summary: Standalone experiment testing whether an oracle slot teacher improves a QLoRA-adapted `Qwen/Qwen3-4B` numeric-copy compiler on long modular-arithmetic programs.
title: Qwen Teacher-Distilled Slot Compiler

summary: This experiment tests whether a frozen Qwen encoder attached to a structured latent executor can keep using a learned program interface after symbol-trace supervision is removed.
title: Qwen Trace Bootstrap Retention Experiment

summary: This standalone experiment tests whether a local 4B model can compile natural language tasks into explicit executable procedures over a fixed stack ABI.
title: Qwen Trace Procedure Depth Stress

summary: Standalone experiment for a Qwen-oriented typed-bytecode training loop. The experiment tests whether exact execution and answer-verified program search can turn a weak text-to-bytecode compiler into a stronger deployable compiler.
title: Qwen Typed Bytecode Expert Iteration

summary: Standalone experiment testing whether train-only verified transformation examples help Qwen solve public text-transformation tasks more consistently.
title: Qwen Verified Skill Memory RAG

summary: Standalone experiment testing whether exact-answer verifier search can repair near-miss compiled programs from a QLoRA-adapted `Qwen/Qwen3-4B` numeric-copy compiler.
title: Qwen Verifier-Guided Slot Repair

summary: This standalone experiment trains a Qwen-attached VM agent. The agent receives a natural-language task, an initial typed bytecode program, and VM observations. At each private turn it emits one textual edit action or `STOP`; the VM executes the edit and returns the updated program and execution trace as text.
title: Qwen VM-Agent ECHO QLoRA

summary: This experiment tests whether a frozen-Qwen bytecode compiler improves when it is trained not only to emit a target program, but also to predict the VM observations produced by that program.
title: Qwen VM-ECHO Trace Distillation

summary: Standalone no-training experiment for deterministic transformation ABI coverage with counterexample filtering.
title: real_transform_abi_gate_with_counterexamples

summary: This directory contains the small, download-friendly artifacts for a standalone rule-family diversity scaling experiment.
title: Rule-Family Diversity Scaling Experiment

summary: This experiment tests latent recurrent execution over correlated belief states when training supervision is limited to one sampled final query value per example.
title: Sampled Query Filter Executor Experiment

summary: This experiment tests whether exact modular belief-state execution is recovered when the recurrent state is an explicit sparse set of support slots rather than a fixed-width dense vector.
title: Sparse Support Memory Executor Experiment

summary: This experiment tests which initializer structure is sufficient to populate a sparse modular belief support before an exact recurrent transition executes the program.
title: Structured Slot Initializer Ladder Experiment

summary: Standalone experiment package for testing whether fixed-budget targeted bridge allocation improves trace-conditioned recombination repair compared with uniform bridge allocation.
title: Targeted Bridge Allocation

summary: This directory contains the small, download-friendly artifacts for a standalone trace-conditioned repair experiment.
title: Trace-Keyed Symbol Repair Experiment
